topics.titleTr: Bülten Büyütme ve Gelir 2026: Sıfırdan Rehber
topics.titleEn: Grow and Monetize a Newsletter in 2026
topics.angle: guide + grow an email list and turn it into revenue as the core of a creator business
topics.brief: Data-led: the newsletter as a creator business's core. Sections: why owned email beats rented reach; growth tactics (lead magnets, referrals, cross-promo); the monetization ladder (services → sponsorships → digital products → paid subs); the free+paid hybrid; the weekly-cadence sweet spot. Claims to verify: 28B emails / 255M readers in 2025, 41%+ open rates, paid-sub revenue $8M→$19M YoY, median 66 days to first dollar. Primary sources: beehiiv.com State of Newsletters 2026; newsletter.supply monetization guide. End with a 90-day launch-to-first-dollar plan.

topics.titleTr: AI ile Shopify Ürün Açıklamaları Yazmak
topics.titleEn: Writing Shopify Product Descriptions with AI
topics.angle: how-to + write AI product descriptions that actually convert and get cited in AI search
topics.brief: Contrarian: bulk AI descriptions won't lift sales on their own. Sections: why generic AI copy hurts both conversion and GEO; a prompt that feeds real attributes, benefits, and objections; Shopify Magic vs ChatGPT vs Jasper; photo-to-spec generation; the draft-plus-human-edit loop; structuring copy so AI search can cite it. Claims to verify: better descriptions lift conversion 10%+ (some studies up to 23.7%); Shopify Magic reads uploaded photos; AI+human beats either alone. Primary sources: easyappsecom.com Shopify AI descriptions guide; stormy.ai Shopify GEO playbook. End with a pre-publish QA checklist.

topics.titleTr: Gemini Gems ile İşletmene Özel AI Asistanları
topics.titleEn: Gemini Gems: Custom AI Assistants for Your Business
topics.angle: how-to + build reusable Gemini Gems so a small business stops re-typing the same prompts
topics.brief: Field-notes: building reusable Gemini Gems for a small business. Sections: what a Gem is (saved expert instructions for repeatable tasks); premade business Gems (Marketing insights, Sales pitch ideator, Hiring consultant, Outreach specialist); building a custom Gem step by step; Opal mini-apps and the Google Business Profile connection; where Gems beat one-off prompts and where they don't. Claims to verify: the premade Workspace Gems list, Opal integrated into the Gemini web app, the Business Profile connector. Primary sources: workspace.google.com Gemini Gems business post; gemini.google/overview/gems. End with three clone-ready Gem recipes.

topics.titleTr: NotebookLM ile Araştırma ve Öğrenme Rehberi
topics.titleEn: NotebookLM for Research and Study: A How-To
topics.angle: how-to + use NotebookLM as a source-grounded second brain for study and research
topics.brief: Question-led: can NotebookLM be your second brain? Sections: source-grounded answers with citations; uploading PDFs, docs, and links; Audio and Video Overviews; the Learning Guide, flashcards, and quizzes for students; 2026 Deep Research + Chat Artifacts that run code and build reports/slides; limits and verification. Claims to verify: free with any Google account; the 2026 update adds Gemini 3.5, Deep Research, and Video Overview; it grounds strictly in your uploaded sources. Primary sources: blog.google NotebookLM better-research post; digitalocean.com What is NotebookLM 2026. End with a study-and-research workflow.

topics.titleTr: AI Akıllı Gözlükler 2026: Meta ve Android XR
topics.titleEn: AI Smart Glasses 2026: Meta vs Android XR
topics.angle: comparison + map the 2026 AI smart-glasses race and who should buy now vs wait
topics.brief: Data-led comparison: the 2026 AI smart-glasses race. Sections: the Meta Ray-Ban Display ($799, Neural Band EMG) and Meta's own $299 Adventurer/Fury line; Google+Samsung Android XR audio glasses (Warby Parker, Gentle Monster) shipping fall 2026 with Gemini; a capability table (display vs audio, assistant, live translation, navigation); who should wait. Claims to verify: Meta ~80% market share, the ~10M unit threshold in 2026, the Android XR Gemini feature set, price points. Primary sources: androidcentral.com Android XR vs Ray-Ban Meta; the-gadgeteer.com best smart glasses 2026. End with a buyer's decision guide.

topics.titleTr: AI Çağında İlk 10 Müşteri: Solo Kurucu Rehberi
topics.titleEn: Your First 10 Customers as a Solo AI Founder
topics.angle: guide + get from an AI-built MVP to first paying customers when building is the easy part
topics.brief: Story-led: from AI-built MVP to first paying customers. Sections: why the build is now the easy part; scoping an AI-workflow product you can ship solo in 4-6 weeks; charging on day one; landing the first 10 customers through direct outreach, not SEO or ads; validating price; when the AI stack stops carrying you and what to hire first. Claims to verify: 63% of Q2 2026 Stripe Atlas C-corps were solo-founded; AI-augmented solo founders earn ~3x; the Base44 arc (0→$3.5M ARR→$80M exit). Primary sources: solofounders.com Stripe stat; fortune.com solo founders AI. End with a first-10-customers playbook.

topics.titleTr: Gemini 3 Deep Think Nedir? Derin Akıl Yürütme
topics.titleEn: What Is Gemini 3 Deep Think? Deeper Reasoning
topics.angle: deep-dive + explain what Deep Think does and when the extra reasoning is worth it
topics.brief: Question-led: when do you actually need Gemini 3 Deep Think? Sections: what Deep Think is (iterative, multi-round reasoning for hard math, science, and logic); how it differs from default Gemini and from quick answers; the latency and cost tradeoff; the Google AI Ultra gating ($100+/mo); real tasks where it wins vs where it's overkill. Claims to verify: Deep Think uses iterative reasoning rounds; it is gated to AI Ultra; Ultra starts at $100/mo. Primary sources: gemini.google/release-notes; blog.google Google AI updates June 2026. End with a 'reach for Deep Think when…' decision list.

topics.titleTr: Claude Skills Nedir? Herkes İçin Pratik Rehber
topics.titleEn: Claude Skills Explained: A Guide for Everyone
topics.angle: how-to + show non-developers how Claude Skills package repeatable work and when to use them
topics.brief: Field-notes: Claude Skills for non-developers. Sections: what a Skill is (packaged instructions Claude loads when a task matches); how Skills differ from raw prompts and Projects; enabling and invoking them; practical Skills for writers, marketers, and analysts; sharing Skills across a team; where Skills help vs add noise. Key claims to verify: Skills are reusable instruction packages Claude auto-triggers by task; they work across Claude apps; they are distinct from MCP connectors. Primary sources: docs.claude.com Skills documentation; anthropic.com/news Skills coverage. End with three copy-ready non-developer Skills.

topics.angle: guide + use WhatsApp as a compliant marketing and retention channel for SMBs
topics.brief: Field-notes: WhatsApp as a marketing channel, especially in Turkey and emerging markets. Sections: the WhatsApp Business app vs the Business Platform (API); catalogs, quick replies, labels, and broadcast lists vs the 24-hour session and template-message rules; opt-in and consent; abandoned-cart and order-update flows; lightweight AI auto-replies. Key claims to verify: template messages are required outside the 24-hour customer-service window; catalog and cart features live in the Business app; opt-in is mandatory. Primary sources: business.whatsapp.com Platform docs; a 2026 WhatsApp marketing guide. End with a compliant broadcast checklist.

topics.titleTr: Kod Yazmadan AI Müşteri Destek Botu Kurmak
topics.titleEn: Build an AI Support Chatbot Without Code
topics.keywordEn: ai customer support chatbot no code
topics.angle: how-to + stand up a grounded, no-code AI support bot for a small store without hallucinated promises
topics.brief: Field-notes: a no-code AI support bot for a small store. Sections: building a knowledge base from FAQs, policies, and docs; picking a no-code platform (site widget, WhatsApp, Instagram DM); grounding answers to avoid hallucinated promises; human-handoff triggers; measuring deflection and CSAT; guardrails on refunds and pricing. Key claims to verify: retrieval-grounded bots reduce invented commitments; a human-handoff path raises satisfaction; deflection rate is the core ROI metric. Primary sources: a 2026 no-code AI chatbot guide; vendor docs such as Intercom Fin or Tidio. End with a launch checklist and escalation rules.

topics.titleTr: Kısa Videoda İlk 3 Saniye: 7 Hook Hatası
topics.titleEn: The First 3 Seconds: 7 Short-Video Hook Mistakes
topics.angle: mistakes + fix the first-three-seconds hook errors that quietly kill short-video reach
topics.brief: Contrarian/mistakes: your Reels don't fail on editing, they fail in the first three seconds. Sections: seven hook mistakes (slow intros, burying the payoff, weak visual motion, no on-screen text, a vague promise, wrong aspect for saves, ignoring the retention graph); reading the retention curve; scripting a hook that earns the next two seconds; hooks that lift saves and shares. Key claims to verify: watch-through/retention and saves drive short-video distribution; the opening seconds decide reach. Primary sources: a 2026 short-form retention study; TikTok and Instagram creator docs. End with a seven-point hook checklist.

topics.titleTr: AI Video Üretimi 2026: Sora, Veo ve Fazlası
topics.titleEn: AI Video Generation 2026: Sora vs Veo Compared
topics.angle: comparison + walk through making a short video with today's AI tools and their limits
topics.brief: Story-led: making a short video with AI in 2026. Sections: the current field (OpenAI Sora, Google Veo, Runway, Kling); text-to-video vs image-to-video; strengths, clip length, audio, and cost; watermarking and provenance (C2PA, SynthID); a realistic creator workflow; where AI video still breaks. Key claims to verify: Veo lives in the Gemini/Google ecosystem and Sora is OpenAI's; SynthID/C2PA labeling on generated video; per-clip length limits. Primary sources: deepmind.google Veo page; openai.com Sora page. End with a pick-your-tool table.

topics.titleTr: X'te Kişisel Marka: Kurucular İçin Büyüme
topics.titleEn: Building a Personal Brand on X for Founders
topics.angle: guide + build a founder brand on X that converts attention into subscribers, not just followers
topics.brief: Contrarian: follower count is a vanity trap—build an X brand that converts. Sections: why niche credibility beats raw reach; a content mix (build-in-public, insights, contrarian takes, threads); the link-in-reply pattern for reach; using bookmarks and saves as the real signal; consistency over virality; turning attention into email subscribers. Key claims to verify: X deprioritizes posts with external links in the body (the link-in-reply workaround); saves/bookmarks and dwell weigh heavily; long-term consistency compounds. Primary sources: a 2026 X algorithm/growth analysis; founder case studies. End with a 30-day founder posting plan.

topics.titleTr: Yerel SEO 2026: Google Business Profil Kontrol Listesi
topics.titleEn: Local SEO 2026: A Google Business Profile Checklist
topics.angle: checklist + optimize a Google Business Profile to win the map pack and AI local answers
topics.brief: Data-led checklist: local SEO in 2026. Sections: Google Business Profile optimization (categories, services, photos, posts, reviews, Q&A); NAP consistency and citations; review velocity and responses; local landing pages and schema; the map pack vs AI Overviews and Gemini's Business Profile connection; tracking calls and directions. Key claims to verify: reviews plus proximity/relevance drive map-pack ranking; Gemini can connect to a Google Business Profile; AI answers increasingly surface local results. Primary sources: Google Business Profile help docs; a 2026 local SEO guide. End with a 15-point GBP audit checklist.

topics.titleTr: Telefonunda Yapay Zeka: Cihaz İçi AI 2026
topics.titleEn: AI on Your Phone: On-Device AI in 2026
topics.angle: deep-dive + explain what actually runs locally on your phone and why it matters
topics.brief: Contrarian deep-dive: your AI's future isn't only in the cloud—it's on your phone. Sections: what on-device AI is (small models running locally); Apple Intelligence, Gemini Nano, and Galaxy AI; the privacy and offline wins; the latency and battery limits; hybrid on-device-plus-cloud routing; what truly runs locally today vs the marketing. Key claims to verify: Gemini Nano runs on-device on Pixel/Android; Apple Intelligence blends on-device with Private Cloud Compute; small models handle summarize/reply/transcribe locally. Primary sources: Google Android on-device AI docs; Apple Intelligence overview. End with what to expect from a 2026 flagship phone.

topics.titleTr: AI ile CV Yazarken 6 Hata (ve ATS Tuzağı)
topics.titleEn: 6 Mistakes Using AI for Your Resume (and ATS)
topics.angle: mistakes + use AI to tailor a resume without tripping ATS or losing your voice
topics.brief: Question-led/mistakes: is AI helping or hurting your job search? Sections: six mistakes (generic AI-written resumes, keyword stuffing for ATS, fabricated metrics, a cover letter that loses your voice, ignoring the job description, one resume for every role); using ChatGPT to tailor rather than generate; ATS-friendly formatting; verifying every claim. Key claims to verify: ATS parsing rewards clean formatting and real keyword matches; recruiters detect generic AI copy; tailoring beats mass-applying. Primary sources: a 2026 ATS/resume guide; LinkedIn or hiring-platform data. End with a tailor-in-15-minutes workflow.

topics.titleTr: AI ile Dijital Ürün Satışı: Pasif Gelir Rehberi
topics.titleEn: Selling Digital Products with AI: A Passive Income Guide
topics.angle: guide + build and sell digital products with AI, and where 'passive' income really breaks
topics.brief: Story-led: earning with digital products built using AI. Sections: what sells (templates, Notion systems, prompt packs, ebooks, mini-courses); validating demand before building; using AI to produce and package fast; pricing and platforms (Gumroad, Lemon Squeezy, Etsy); distribution via newsletter and social; the honest ceiling of 'passive'. Key claims to verify: digital products rank among top creator/newsletter revenue models; they are cheap to produce with AI; distribution, not creation, is the real bottleneck. Primary sources: a 2026 digital-products/creator-economy report; Gumroad creator data. End with a first-product launch checklist.
